content: For WebSocket relay servers in Node.js 2025: ws@8.21.0 is the standard (80M weekly downloads), minimal RFC-6455 compliant, best performance-to-simplicity ratio. uWebSockets.js handles 3-8x more concurrent connections via C++ bindings but adds native dependency complexity. Socket.IO adds rooms/namespaces/auto-reconnect/fallback but requires both client and server to use the Socket.IO library — incompatible with raw WebSocket clients. For a daemon relay where you control both ends, ws is sufficient and simpler; uWebSockets.js only matters at tens of thousands of concurrent connections.
